title: Orange Pi A733 BSP v5p3x threshold and start-DMA audit
trigger: After the public/community repo audit exposed full BSP MMC sources, check whether v5p3x threshold or start-DMA logic reveals one specific, testable Linux delta for the A733 SDMMC0 IDMAC descriptor-fetch stall.
findings: BSP v5p3x sets host->dma_tl to (0x2 << 28) | (7 << 16) | 248, host->idma_des_size_bits to 12, host->sunxi_mmc_thld_ctl to sunxi_mmc_thld_ctl_for_sdmmc_v5p3x(), and host->des_addr_shift to 2 when SMCV reports v5p3 or newer.

The descriptor setup still uses the known normal-IDMAC format: OWN | CH | DIC on each descriptor, shifted data buffer addresses, shifted next-descriptor addresses, FD on the first descriptor, LD on the last descriptor, DIC cleared on the last descriptor, then wmb().

The start-DMA sequence remains the same visible sequence already tested locally: initialize descriptors, reset FIFO, reset DMA interface, reset IDMAC control, set GCTRL DMA enable, enable receive interrupt for reads, then write DMAC FIX_BURST | IDMA_ON.

The v5p3x threshold helper differs from the local diagnostic shim. It preserves existing THLD bits, clears/sets WR_THLD for writes, and only enables RD_THLD for high-speed read timings. For data == NULL it sets both RD_THLD and WR_THLD with a 512-byte threshold.

This threshold delta is not a strong descriptor-fetch candidate. The failure signature is before descriptor word 0 is consumed: IDST=0x4000 DESC_READ, CHDA=DLBA, CBDA=0, CBCR=0x400, BBCR=0, and descriptor OWN unchanged. FIFO threshold policy should affect data movement after descriptor fetch, not the initial memory read of descriptor word 0.
Existing register evidence already includes THLD values seen as 0x02000001 and 0x02000005 in different phases; neither has been tied to descriptor consumption.
reason: The only new BSP-source delta is threshold policy, and it is not credible enough to justify another kernel build/boot against a pre-descriptor-fetch stall. Descriptor geometry, address shifting, DMA mask, reset order, and normal IDMAC enable path remain already covered.
next: Keep waiting for maintainer/list feedback, public archive visibility, or a source-backed SDMMC0 master/firewall/security/firmware handoff bit.
Do not retest THLD-only variants unless a maintainer or vendor source specifically links THLD bit 2 or v5p3x threshold gating to IDMAC descriptor fetch.
